summary: Small single-storey rectangular building with a raised flat roof and a surrounding low ledge. Exterior walls use a lower band of light sandstone-like blocks, a middle row of windows, a top band of smooth white blocks, a thin brick trim, and stone slab coping around the roof. A white low border/planter surrounds the structure on ground level. Visible interior is empty or recessed below roof line.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with a recessed flat roof and perimeter low ledge
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical on long axis (appears similar on both longer facades)

elements: continuous horizontal windows (row of 4-5 on each longer side)
flat recessed roof with stone slab coping
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular single-storey box about 9x13 blocks footprint and ~5 blocks tall to top of coping.
Use sandstone (or similar light textured block) for the lower two or three block rows, place a horizontal row of glass windows centered above it (4 windows on visible facades).
Above windows place a smooth white block row (quartz) one block tall, then a 1-block-tall brick band, and cap with stone slabs around the roof edge.

Make the roof interior filled with red-tinted flat blocks (brick pattern visible) recessed one block below the coping. Surround building at ground level with a one-block-high white border/ledge separated by a one-block gap from the wall.
uncertainties: Exact block IDs: sandstone appears likely but could be smooth_sandstone; quartz is inferred for white smooth band but could be another white block.
Roof infill material is red/brick textured in top view; could be brick or red terracotta/concrete—uncertain.
Precise footprint dimensions: estimated from visible window count and top views; corners and small offsets may differ by 1-2 blocks.
